description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 11: Fun with Symmetry

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of shapes and equal halves, can fast-track to symmetry concepts with challenge content

videoSequence.reason: Fun symmetry song to reinforce concepts
Find lines of symmetry in different shapes
Challenge: Paper folding and completing figures

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of symmetry concepts

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in shape recognition or equal halves, needs targeted review before symmetry concepts

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Start main topic: Line of symmetry

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in shapes and equal halves before attempting symmetry

videoSequence.reason: Gentle intro to line of symmetry
Fun symmetry song to build interest

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with symmetry.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

equal_halves.conceptName: Understanding equal halves and matching parts

matching_shapes.conceptName: Matching identical shapes and spotting same-different

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: A shape has a line of symmetry when you can fold it and both halves ___.
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry divides a shape into two halves that match perfectly when folded.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: two through opposite corners and two through the middle of opposite sides.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand lines of symmetry well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with symmetry concept, review equal halves

questions.question: Which of these shapes is NOT symmetrical?
options: A shape with all unequal sides
questions.explanation: A shape with all unequal sides (a scalene figure) is not symmetrical. Circles, squares, and rectangles all have lines of symmetry.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for folding and completing figures challenge

ifWrong.message: Watch the symmetrical shapes video again, then try spotting symmetry in objects around your room.

skip_ahead.action: Skip song/reinforcement video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on shapes and halves

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising symmetry with paper folding activities at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your shapes and halves really well. You are ready to learn about symmetry. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand shapes and equal halves well. Let's explore the fun world of symmetry with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in shapes or equal halves. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to symmetry.

low_score.message: You need to review shapes and equal halves first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn symmetry.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how symmetry concepts build on each other

concepts: Symmetrical shapes and their lines of symmetry

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

symmetryIdentification.description: % of students who can correctly identify lines of symmetry in basic shapes

mirrorImageAccuracy.description: % of students who can draw a correct mirror image of a simple figure

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 30-35 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (20 min) + retake + main content (20 min) = 40-50 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing line of symmetry with any line drawn on a shape
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise that both halves must match exactly when folded along the line. Use paper folding to demonstrate.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students thinking mirror images are identical copies rather than reflections
commonChallenges.solution2: Use a real mirror to show how left and right flip. Let students hold up letters and objects.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students struggling to complete a symmetrical figure on a grid
commonChallenges.solution3: Start with simple shapes on large grids. Count squares from the line of symmetry to place dots correctly.
